evidence: goseg/startram/startram.go defines package globals (`EventBus`, `Regions`) and mutates them from request functions (`GetRegions`, `Retrieve`), creating hidden cross-module stateful behavior.

goseg/startram/startram.go `Retrieve` mutates `config.StartramConfig` and persists config via `config.UpdateConf`, so a transport-facing module writes directly into another module’s global/runtime state.

goseg/config/version.go `CheckVersion` combines remote HTTP fetch, global state mutation (`VersionInfo`, `VersionServerReady`), and local file persistence (`settings/version_info.json`) in one boundary-crossing function.

README.md defines a quality gate only for frontend/runtime changes (npm ci/test/build) and does not define any required backend checks for Go modules that implement runtime-critical network/config behavior.

goseg/startram/startram.go and goseg/config/version.go use hardwired network/time dependencies (http.Get/http.Post/http.DefaultClient/time.Sleep) and side effects, with no boundary interfaces in these files for deterministic isolation tests.
goseg/handler/upload.go routes privileged actions (open-endpoint/reset) directly to importer operations but has no contract-level validation hooks or documented test requirement in README.md.

Public API error contracts are inconsistent across adjacent modules: `config.CheckVersion()` returns `(structs.Channel, bool)` without an error object, `config.LocalVersion()` panics on read/decode failures, while `handler.UploadHandler()` and most `startram` functions return `error` (`goseg/config/version.go:23`, `goseg/config/version.go:121`, `goseg/handler/upload.go:12`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:39`).

Similar StarTram API functions handle HTTP response semantics differently: `Register`/`SvcCreate`/`SvcDelete`/`AliasCreate` do not check `resp.StatusCode`, but `GetBackup` and `UploadBackup` fail on non-200 status (`goseg/startram/startram.go:123`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:172`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:455`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:486`).

API signatures include overloaded boolean flags and unused parameters that make call contracts ambiguous, e.g. `RestoreBackup(ship, remote, timestamp, md5hash, dev, bakType)` branch-selects behavior via booleans, and `GetBackup(..., backupPassword, ...)` declares `backupPassword` but does not use it (`goseg/startram/startram.go:455`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:604`).

Several externally callable functions have hidden global side effects not reflected in signatures: `Retrieve()` mutates `config.StartramConfig` and pushes to `EventBus`, `GetRegions()` mutates global `Regions`, `CheckVersion()` mutates global version state and writes disk (`goseg/startram/startram.go:64`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:98`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:117`, `goseg/config/version.go:77`, `goseg/config/version.go:80`).

findings.summary: Cross-module coordination relies on mutable package globals, creating hidden coupling and broad change blast radius.
evidence: goseg/startram/startram.go:33-36 defines mutable globals (`EventBus`, `Regions`), and `GetRegions` overwrites `Regions` at line 64.
goseg/startram/startram.go:98 writes into external package global `config.StartramConfig` during `Retrieve`.
goseg/config/version.go:17-20 defines mutable globals (`VersionServerReady`, `VersionInfo`) and `CheckVersion` mutates/returns them as implicit shared state (lines 46-47, 77, 94-95).
findings.suggestion: Introduce explicit state owners with interfaces (e.g., `StartramStore`, `VersionStore`) and inject them into call sites; replace package-global mutable variables with encapsulated structs guarded by synchronization where needed.

findings.summary: Network transport logic is tightly mixed with config persistence and event emission, collapsing module boundaries.
evidence: goseg/startram/startram.go `Retrieve` performs HTTP, decodes payload, mutates config state (`config.UpdateConf` at lines 107-109), and emits module event (`EventBus <- ...` at line 117) in one flow.
goseg/config/version.go `CheckVersion` performs HTTP polling and also writes a persisted JSON snapshot to disk (`os.Create` line 80, encoder writes lines 88-90).

goseg/handler/upload.go delegates directly to importer session mutation (`importer.SetUploadSession`, `importer.Reset`) from websocket action dispatch, without an intermediate application service boundary.
findings.suggestion: Split responsibilities into explicit layers: API client layer for HTTP calls, domain service layer for registration/version workflows, and persistence/event adapters; handlers should call service interfaces, not storage/session primitives directly.

findings.summary: Startram and config modules are mutually entangled through reads and writes, weakening dependency direction.
evidence: goseg/startram/startram.go depends on config for runtime reads (`config.Conf()` throughout) and writes (`config.UpdateConf`, `config.StartramConfig` mutation).

goseg/config/version.go maintains its own remote-fetch orchestration and global status state rather than depending on a dedicated outbound service abstraction, reinforcing horizontal coupling between runtime config and external API concerns.
findings.suggestion: Define a narrow configuration interface consumed by startram (read/update methods only) and move external API orchestration into dedicated service packages so config remains a state provider instead of a peer orchestrator.

findings.summary: Startram service operations collapse transport, state mutation, and event publication into single entrypoints, creating hidden cross-module contracts.
evidence: Retrieve updates config.StartramConfig, conditionally writes wgRegistered via config.UpdateConf, and emits EventBus events after remote API reads.
Register/SvcDelete/AliasCreate call remote APIs and then trigger Retrieve-driven state synchronization, coupling endpoint behavior to local config/event semantics.
README.md documents frontend ownership/contracts but no backend module-boundary contract for Startram side effects.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a Startram boundary layer that separates API client, state reconciliation, and event emission into distinct interfaces; document the backend boundary contract in README so callers depend on explicit outputs rather than side effects.

findings.summary: Package-level mutable state in configuration/version and Startram modules creates non-local coupling and broad blast radius for behavior changes.
evidence: goseg/config/version.go exposes and mutates VersionInfo and VersionServerReady globally in CheckVersion.
goseg/startram/startram.go exposes EventBus and Regions as globals and mutates them inside request paths.
These globals are updated during I/O-heavy flows, making behavior depend on call ordering across modules rather than explicit contracts.
findings.suggestion: Encapsulate mutable runtime state behind owned structs with constructor-injected dependencies; return immutable result objects to callers and publish events through an interface-owned dispatcher.

findings.summary: Documented quality gates cover frontend only, leaving critical Go runtime paths without an enforced verification contract.
evidence: README.md Frontend Quality Gate requires npm ci/test/build but defines no required Go test/lint/integration gate.
startram.go handles registration lifecycle, retries, and backup transfer behavior that can alter system state and external registration state.
Without a documented backend gate, these critical paths can change without mandatory contract verification.
findings.suggestion: Add a backend quality gate section in README requiring targeted Go unit/integration tests for config/version, startram registration flows, and upload handler behavior before merge/release.

findings.summary: Hardcoded HTTP/time/filesystem dependencies in critical functions make deterministic contract tests difficult and encourage under-testing.
evidence: CheckVersion directly uses http.DefaultClient.Do, retry sleeps, and direct file writes to settings/version_info.json in one flow.
Startram functions use direct http.Get/http.Post and internal retry/backoff loops (backoffRetrieve) without injected clock/client.
UploadHandler directly invokes importer.SetUploadSession/importer.Reset based on action strings, with no local seam for controlled behavioral tests.
findings.suggestion: Refactor these modules to accept injected HTTP client, clock/sleeper, and persistence/action interfaces, then add table-driven tests for success/failure/timeout branches and action validation.

findings.summary: Adjacent public APIs expose conflicting failure contracts (boolean status, panic, and error return), making callers handle similar operations in incompatible ways.
evidence: `CheckVersion()` returns `(structs.Channel, bool)` and suppresses concrete error reasons (`goseg/config/version.go:23`).
`LocalVersion()` panics on file I/O and JSON decode errors (`goseg/config/version.go:131`, `goseg/config/version.go:137`, `goseg/config/version.go:142`).
`UploadHandler()` and StarTram endpoints return `error` values for similar external failures (`goseg/handler/upload.go:12`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:123`).
findings.suggestion: Standardize exported runtime APIs on `(..., error)` (or `error` only), replace panic paths with propagated errors, and reserve panics for truly unrecoverable startup invariants.

findings.summary: StarTram methods that represent the same API layer disagree on whether HTTP status codes are part of the contract, causing inconsistent success/failure behavior.
evidence: `Register`, `SvcCreate`, `SvcDelete`, and `AliasCreate` parse response JSON without checking `resp.StatusCode` (`goseg/startram/startram.go:136`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:185`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:223`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:260`).
`GetBackup` and `UploadBackup` explicitly fail when status is non-200 (`goseg/startram/startram.go:475`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:528`).
README documents operational usage but does not define API failure semantics, leaving this inconsistency unbounded for consumers (`README.md`).
findings.suggestion: Define one transport contract for all StarTram calls (status-first, then payload decode), implement a shared request helper enforcing it, and document expected failure categories.

findings.summary: Exported functions use overloaded boolean switches and dead parameters, producing ambiguous and misleading interfaces.
evidence: `RestoreBackup(ship string, remote bool, timestamp int, md5hash string, dev bool, bakType string)` multiplexes distinct flows through booleans (`goseg/startram/startram.go:604`).
`GetBackup(ship, timestamp, backupPassword, pubkey, endpointUrl string)` includes `backupPassword` but never uses it (`goseg/startram/startram.go:455`).
This contrasts with cleaner single-purpose config APIs (`CreateDefaultVersion() error`), highlighting inconsistent public shape expectations in nearby modules (`goseg/config/version.go:102`).
findings.suggestion: Split overloaded functions into explicit operations (e.g., `RestoreRemoteBackup`, `RestoreLocalBackup`, `RestoreDevBackup`), remove unused parameters, and pass typed option structs only when multiple modes are truly required.

findings.summary: Read-oriented function names hide state mutation and event emission, violating interface honesty and making API behavior hard to predict.
evidence: `Retrieve()` updates `config.StartramConfig`, conditionally writes config (`UpdateConf`), and emits `EventBus` events while appearing as a fetch call (`goseg/startram/startram.go:98`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:107`, `goseg/startram/startram.go:117`).
`GetRegions()` updates global `Regions` map in addition to returning data (`goseg/startram/startram.go:64`).
`CheckVersion()` both fetches and persists `version_info.json`, plus mutates globals (`goseg/config/version.go:77`, `goseg/config/version.go:80`).
findings.suggestion: Separate pure retrieval methods from mutation/event methods (e.g., `Fetch*` vs `Sync*`), or make side effects explicit in names and docs to keep call semantics predictable.
